<!-- docgen:version=0.1.1 reason: cosmetic refresh; hook script and responsibilities unchanged -->

## Purpose
Holds the `pre-commit` hook script that `install.sh` wires into a repo (via `core.hooksPath` or `pre-commit-local`). It is a thin launcher that invokes `scrub.py --staged` and fails OPEN on tool errors so a broken scrubber never bricks commits.

## Files
- `pre-commit` — bash hook. Resolves repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, locates `tools/secret-scrub/scrub.py`, and runs `python3 scrub.py --staged`. Exits 1 only when scrub.py reports rc=1 (residual secret); any other rc — missing `python3`, missing script, internal error — exits 0 to let the commit through.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `../scrub.py` — the actual scanner/redactor; this script is just its launcher.
- `../install.sh` — the only supported way to install this file into a repo's hook path.
- `git` CLI — used for `rev-parse --show-toplevel`.

## Gotchas
- Fails OPEN on purpose: missing `python3`, missing `scrub.py`, or any non-0/non-1 rc exits 0. The contract is "never brick commits over a tool bug" — only rc=1 (residual secret after scrub) blocks.
- Do not add machine-global side effects here; this file runs in a user's repo and the broader subsystem promises per-repo-only behaviour.
